steps.title: Find the discriminant
steps.content:
For $3x^2-12x-5=0$, the coefficients are $a=3$, $b=-12$, and $c=-5$. The expression under the radical in the quadratic formula is the discriminant.
$$b^2-4ac=(-12)^2-4(3)(-5)=144+60=204$$

steps.title: Match the radical value
steps.content:
The quadratic formula gives
$$x=\frac{12\pm\sqrt{204}}{6}$$
The form $\frac{12-\sqrt{k}}{6}$ therefore has $k=204$. The answer is 204.
